title: Soft Body Puppet Theater
description: A soft-body puppet theater where Kira and a rigged humanoid figure are connected by elastic jelly-like tethers. The user clicks and drags anchor points on each character to deform their soft-body mesh envelopes, stretch the tethers between them, and trigger wobble physics cascades. Achieving specific deformation targets across both characters completes the choreography.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a soft-body puppet theater. Load both shared_assets/kira.glb and shared_assets/RiggedFigure.glb using GLTFLoader. Position Kira at (-2, 0, 0) and RiggedFigure at (2, 0, 0) on a stage-like ground plane with a dark wooden texture. Wrap each loaded model inside a translucent soft-body envelope mesh (a slightly enlarged copy of their bounding geometry using SphereGeometry subdivided 32x32, with MeshPhysicalMaterial, transmission 0.3, roughness 0.1, color tinted pink for Kira and blue for RiggedFigure). Implement spring-mass soft-body simulation on each envelope: store vertex positions as particles with velocity, apply spring forces between neighboring vertices (stiffness 0.4, damping 0.02), and gravity. Connect the two envelopes with 4 visible elastic tether lines (CatmullRomCurve3 rendered as TubeGeometry) that stretch and contract based on distance. The user interaction requires 8 steps: (1) click-drag Kira's envelope to deform it leftward past a threshold of 0.5 units, (2) click-drag RiggedFigure's envelope upward past 0.8 units, (3) stretch a tether between them beyond 5 units by dragging both apart, (4) compress them together until tether length is below 1.5 units, (5) poke Kira's envelope from above to trigger a wobble amplitude exceeding 0.3, (6) poke RiggedFigure from the side to achieve lateral wobble above 0.4, (7) drag both envelopes simultaneously to align their centers vertically within 0.2 units tolerance, (8) release both to let them settle with residual oscillation below 0.05. Use raycaster for mouse picking. Validate each step with parameter checks and display a step counter HUD in an HTML overlay showing current step and target. Add three-point lighting: warm key light, cool fill light, and rim light. Set camera at (0, 3, 8) looking at origin with OrbitControls. Animate at 60fps updating soft-body physics each frame. Show a completion banner when all 8 steps are validated.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  kiraDeformX: number,         // horizontal deformation of Kira's envelope in units
  figureDeformY: number,         // vertical deformation of RiggedFigure's envelope in units
  maxTetherLength: number,         // maximum stretched length of any tether in units
  kiraWobbleAmplitude: number,         // current wobble amplitude of Kira's envelope
  figureWobbleAmplitude: number,         // current wobble amplitude of RiggedFigure's envelope
  tetherStretchCount: number,         // count of tethers stretched beyond threshold
  activeAnchorPoints: number,         // count of currently dragged anchor points
  simultaneousDeform: boolean,         // true when both envelopes deformed at same time
  cascadeTriggered: boolean,         // true when wobble cascade propagates between models
  totalInteractions: number,         // cumulative count of drag interactions completed
}
